original_abstract: Community nursing serves as a cornerstone of health promotion and disease prevention in Ecuador, particularly within rural and marginalized urban settings. This study examines the vital contributions of community nurses in the realms of health education, immunization initiatives, and chronic disease management. Findings indicate that targeted educational interventions significantly foster healthier lifestyle habits and reduce the prevalence of childhood malnutrition and gastrointestinal ailments. Furthermore, vaccination campaigns have expanded healthcare reach to historically underserved populations, while longitudinal follow-up programs have been instrumental in enhancing treatment adherence and patient quality of life. Despite these contributions, the field faces substantial hurdles, including inadequate infrastructure, resource scarcities, and limited access to professional development. To amplify the efficacy of community nursing, the study proposes the integration of specialized public policies, the adoption of digital health innovations, and the strengthening of intersectoral partnerships. By addressing these systemic barriers and leveraging identified opportunities, Ecuador can fortify its community nursing workforce and drive meaningful improvements in public health outcomes.
humanized_abstract: Community nursing is one of the main strategies used for health promotion and prevention in Ecuador, particularly in rural and vulnerable areas in urban context. This study aims to demonstrate the influence of community nurses on education, immunization and other aspects of noncommunicable diseases. An assessment that was done in one area of Quito and Tambopaxi in Guayas showed that an appropriate education given to a household could lead to improvement of health behaviours and reduction in stunting and diarrhea among children, as well as improvement in immunization coverages for children and women in marginalized urban and poor communities by means of more frequent vaccination campaigns; also, through the follow up of noncommunicable diseases in some communities it showed a better adherence to therapy and therefore an improvement in the quality of life. However, community nursing still has many challenges and needs to work on the health infrastructure, materials, capacities and thus needs policies of a public nature to achieve a change in the populations, the technological developments within the sector, also among others, the creation of alliances among the sectors involved. Therefore, based on the achievements and gaps faced by community nurses, we propose policies to strengthen and improve the efficiency of this sector to benefit health systems and communities in Ecuador and to reach the expected and necessary improvements at a social level for the citizens and to contribute to a great extent in the reduction and control of disease rates as well as to ensure their health and well-being as a great asset for nations.
